title: epiphyllum hookeri subsp. hookeri
intro: Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. hookeri, also known as the Hooker's epiphyte or Night-blooming cereus, is a type of epiphytic cactus native to the Western Ghats of India. This plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to the presence of flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Epiphyllum hookeri subsp. hookeri include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
